OperationalDesignDomain.desc: The complete set of conditions under which the automated driving system is designed to operate.

Environment.desc: Atmospheric and ambient conditions that affect driving and sensing.

Weather.desc: Overall atmospheric conditions at the time of driving.

Clear.desc: Weather condition with no significant clouds or precipitation.

Cloudy.desc: Weather condition with significant cloud cover.

Rain.desc: Liquid water precipitation in various intensities.

Snow.desc: Frozen water precipitation in the form of snowflakes.

Sleet.desc: Mixed precipitation of rain and snow.

Hail.desc: Precipitation in the form of balls or irregular lumps of ice.

Storm.desc: Violent atmospheric disturbance with strong wind and precipitation.

Illumination.desc: Lighting conditions affecting visibility and sensor performance.

Twilight.desc: Soft glowing light before sunrise and after sunset.

ArtificialLight.desc: Lighting provided by man-made sources such as street lamps.

Precipitation.desc: Intensity level of any moisture falling from the atmosphere.

Fog.desc: Visible mass of condensed water vapor in the atmosphere, reducing visibility.

LightFog.desc: Thin fog with minor visibility reduction.

MediumFog.desc: Moderate fog with significant visibility reduction.

DenseFog.desc: Thick fog with severe visibility reduction.

Temperature.desc: Degree of heat present in the environment and on road surfaces.

Wind.desc: Movement of air, which may affect vehicle stability and sensors.

AirQuality.desc: Chemical and particulate composition of ambient air.

Road.desc: Physical characteristics and conditions of the roadway.

Highway.desc: Major road designed for high-speed through traffic.

UrbanRoad.desc: Road located within a city or urbanized area.

RuralRoad.desc: Road in countryside or low-density areas.

ResidentialRoad.desc: Road within a residential zone with low speed limits.

ServiceRoad.desc: Road providing access to properties or facilities.

RoadGeometry.desc: Horizontal and vertical shape of the road, including curves and slopes.

RoadSurface.desc: Physical condition and texture of the road surface.

Potholes.desc: Road surface with structural defects and holes.

LaneMarkings.desc: Paint or physical markings defining lanes and traffic rules.

RoadConditions.desc: Overall state of the road including defects, construction, and obstacles.

Shoulder.desc: Area beside the main travel lanes for emergency stopping.

Intersection.desc: Point where two or more roads cross or meet.

Bridge.desc: Structure carrying road over an obstacle such as water or valley.

Tunnel.desc: Enclosed passage for road through a hill or mountain.

Traffic.desc: Conditions related to other road users and traffic regulation.

TrafficParticipants.desc: All actors present in the driving environment.

EmergencyVehicle.desc: Vehicle used for emergency services such as police, fire, ambulance.

VulnerableRoadUser.desc: Road users with little or no protection, at high risk in collisions.

Animal.desc: Domestic or wild animals on or near the road.

TrafficFlow.desc: Movement characteristics of traffic over time.

TrafficDensity.desc: Concentration of vehicles per unit length of road.

TrafficRules.desc: Formal rules governing right-of-way, overtaking, turning, etc.

TrafficSigns.desc: Visual signs providing regulatory, warning, or guidance information.

TrafficSignals.desc: Electric signals controlling right-of-way at intersections.

DrivingDynamics.desc: Dynamic behavior of the vehicle during operation.

Speed.desc: Rate of motion of the vehicle.

LateralMovement.desc: Side-to-side motion including lane changes and steering.

Stopping.desc: Conditions and events related to vehicle stopping.

Infrastructure.desc: Fixed physical structures supporting road safety and control.

TrafficLight.desc: Signal device controlling vehicle and pedestrian flow.

TrafficSign.desc: Permanent or temporary sign conveying traffic information.

GuardRail.desc: Barrier preventing vehicles from leaving the roadway.

Crosswalk.desc: Designated area for pedestrians to cross the road.

RailroadCrossing.desc: Point where road and railway intersect.

TemporalConditions.desc: Time-related factors affecting traffic and environment.

TimeOfDay.desc: Time segment during the day (e.g., morning, evening).

Season.desc: Period of the year (spring, summer, autumn, winter).

Holiday.desc: Public or local holiday affecting traffic patterns.

SpecialEvent.desc: Planned event causing abnormal traffic or environment.

LegalRegulatoryConditions.desc: Laws and regulations governing driving in the region.

SpeedLimits.desc: Legally imposed maximum and minimum speeds.

RightOfWayRules.desc: Rules determining which traffic has priority.

OvertakingRules.desc: Rules governing overtaking and passing maneuvers.

ParkingRules.desc: Rules about where and how parking is permitted.

RegionalLegislation.desc: Local or national laws specific to the operating region.

VehicleCapabilities.desc: Performance and functional limits of the subject vehicle.

AutonomyLevel.desc: Level of driving automation according to standards.

SensorSuite.desc: Set of sensors installed on the vehicle.

ActuatorPerformance.desc: Performance limits of steering, throttle, and braking systems.

Perception.desc: System ability to detect and recognize objects and features.

VisibilityRange.desc: Maximum distance at which objects can be detected.

ObstacleDetection.desc: Ability to detect obstacles of various types and sizes.

LaneRecognition.desc: Ability to recognize lane boundaries and markings.

SignRecognition.desc: Ability to detect and interpret traffic signs.

Map.desc: Digital map characteristics used by the automated system.

MapCoverage.desc: Geographic area covered by the map.

MapType.desc: Format and representation type of the map.

LaneLevelMap.desc: Map containing detailed lane-level geometry and attributes.
